name: Natural Gas / LNG
summary: TTF EASED TO €49.95 BUT STRUCTURAL DEFICIT UNCHANGED: Down from €55.80 peak, still +60%. Qatar LNG offline (5-year repair). EU storage 30%. ECB: 3.1% Q2 inflation. Easing on de-escalation hopes, not supply — April 6 deadline could reverse.

current.snapshot_note: As of Apr 2. Henry Hub decoupled from TTF — US has abundant domestic supply, LNG export terminals at capacity.

price_snapshots.delta_context: European gas was at moderate levels
Surged 45% in single day on Ras Laffan shutdown
Further spike after Iranian missiles caused 5-year damage
Pulled back on peace signal but still +85% from pre-crisis. Goldman forecast EUR 55 for April. EU storage at 30% (five-year seasonal low).

signals.content: QatarEnergy halted LNG production at Ras Laffan (world's largest LNG facility, ~20% of global supply) after attacks. European TTF surged 45%.
Iranian missiles damaged LNG Trains 4 & 6 at Ras Laffan. 17% of Qatar LNG capacity destroyed. Estimated $20B annual revenue loss. 5-year repair timeline. This is STRUCTURAL, not temporary.

European TTF surged 70% in March to €54.52/MWh — worst monthly gain since Sep 2021. Driven by Qatar Ras Laffan destruction (17% capacity, 5-year repair) + Hormuz blocking LNG tankers. European storage at 30% (historically low after harsh winter). Emergency measures in 4 European countries: Spain cut VAT from 21% to 10% on energy, Italy allocated €100M support, Slovakia and Slovenia introduced pump rationing. ECB froze rate cuts March 19 and raised inflation forecast. Atlantic Council: crisis 'could trigger European energy crisis' at 2022 levels. If blockade persists through summer refill season (May-Oct), €80-100/MWh realistic — industrial shutdowns begin at that level.

Henry Hub nat gas -4.7% to $2.95 on Monday. US production strong, partially offsetting Gulf disruption. TTF pulled back slightly to €54.18 (-1.9%). European storage still critical at 30%. Goldman base case TTF €72/MWh in Q2. (Sources: yfinance, OilPriceAPI)

Eurozone inflation surged to 2.5% (from 1.9% in February) — energy component exploded to +4.9% (from -3.1%), an 8.0 percentage point swing in one month. Most dramatic energy inflation print since 2022 Russia-Ukraine shock. Shell warned 'Europe could face shortage of fuel as early as April.' EU gas storage at ~30% — lowest seasonal level since before 2022. Some analysts now predict ECB rate HIKES at April and June meetings. Chemical and steel manufacturers imposing surcharges up to 30% with 'permanent deindustrialization' language appearing. After IEA SPR release, only Japan (124 days) meets 90-day reserve requirement — Germany 76 days, France 70, UK 39, Australia 27. TTF pulled back to €53.73 on Pezeshkian peace signal but structural supply loss (Qatar 17% capacity destroyed, 5-year repair) persists. CF Industries -5.6%, Cheniere -3.4% on de-escalation trade. (Sources: Eurostat, Shell, CNBC, Euronews)

CONFIRMED: European TTF gas at EUR 47.51/MWh — down 6.4% daily on de-escalation hopes, but still elevated at +85% from pre-crisis levels at peak. Goldman Sachs forecast EUR 55/MWh for April. EU gas storage at 30%, the five-year seasonal low. To meet winter storage targets, EU needs to inject 60 bcm by December — structurally impossible without Hormuz LNG supplies resuming. Shell's April fuel shortage warning is now playing out — 'we are IN the window Shell warned about.' Slovenia has begun rationing. Spain spending EUR 5B on emergency energy measures. (Sources: TradingEconomics, ICE Endex, Shell)

CONFIRMED: Heating oil +3.7% vs gasoline -7.3% — SPLIT signal revealing structural distillate crisis. Approximately 10% of global gasoil trade (750K bpd) and ~20% of jet fuel trade (400K bpd) transits Hormuz directly (Kpler data). 1.2M bpd of NW European refining capacity offline for scheduled spring maintenance. EIA showed 5.6M barrel draw in distillate stocks. Distillate crisis is structural even if crude prices normalize — the refining capacity and product flow disruption persists independently. (Sources: Kpler, EIA)

TTF eased to €49.95/MWh (from €55.80 peak) — still +60% from pre-crisis. Structural deficit unchanged: Qatar LNG offline, Ras Laffan 5-year repair. EU storage ~30%. ECB projects 3.1% Q2 inflation driven by energy. The easing is driven by de-escalation hopes (Pezeshkian April 1), not supply improvement — if April 6 deadline expires without extension, TTF rebounds. Natural gas-to-fertilizer cascade: QAFCO offline → urea at $750/mt → India Kharif at risk. (Sources: OilPriceAPI, CNBC, ECB projections)

edges.context: Qatar was ~20% of global LNG. 17% capacity destroyed (5yr repair). AQUA 1 attack near Ras Laffan reinforces threat to Qatar energy infrastructure. Force majeure continues.

LNG shipments from Qatar affected by Hormuz closure + toll law. Even if production resumes, exports blocked by insurance costs + IRGC control. 10% of global gasoil and 20% of jet fuel transits Hormuz.
India imports ~60% of LPG. LPG prices hiked. LNG supply disrupted by both Hormuz closure and Ras Laffan damage.

Natural gas is primary feedstock for nitrogen-based fertilizers. Gas price spike + distillate crisis (heating oil +3.7%) = dual cost pressure on fertilizer production. Structural crisis even if crude normalizes.
Oil and gas prices linked. LNG contracts often oil-indexed. Dual energy shock.

trigger_points.mechanism: Would trigger European industrial demand destruction and global fertilizer crisis. India heavily exposed through fertilizer and LPG imports.
